title: Power, Cooling, Specialized Workstations, and Ergonomic Fit · Device Forensics
summary: A realistic power, cooling, specialized workstations, and ergonomic fit ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in power-thermal-workload fit model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Match wattage headroom, rails, connectors, efficiency, form factor, input, protections, GPU transients, and vendor guidance to measured requirements.
Distinguish surge protection, standby and line-interactive UPS behavior, runtime, load, battery health, grounding, and graceful shutdown needs.
Design intake, exhaust, pressure, filtration, cable clearance, ambient, acoustic, sensor, and maintenance controls around observed heat.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Ergonomic Access Fit
steps.instruction:
Order the Ergonomic Access Fit CompTIA A+ support HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Ergonomic Access Fit.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ergonomic Access Fit assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Ergonomic Access Fit, keep Ergonomic Access Fit connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A specialized workstation list ignores measured workload, transient power, thermal path, acoustics, ergonomics, access needs, and failure cost. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Adapt reach, posture, display placement, input, seating, lighting, breaks, alternate controls, and remote setup with the user and qualified guidance where needed.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Ergonomic Access Fit has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

The debug variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Ergonomic Access Fit, keep Ergonomic Access Fit connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Adapt reach, posture, display placement, input, seating, lighting, breaks, alternate controls, and remote setup with the user and qualified guidance where needed.
